description: BMJ App coding rules (Laravel 11 + Vue 3). LLMs must read and follow these before editing any file in this workspace.

rules.title: Named constants
rules.summary: No string or number literals in conditions or comparisons — always use a named constant or config value.

rules.title: Top-level config branching
rules.summary: One if/else at the top decides the whole strategy — never scatter env checks inside handlers or components. Use Laravel config() or Vue environment variables.

rules.title: Route architecture
rules.summary: Backend: Group API routes by feature in routes/api.php. Frontend: Keep all routing logic config-driven inside src/config/index.js -> menuMapping.

rules.title: Auth defense-in-depth
rules.summary: Backend: Apply auth (Sanctum) and RoleMiddleware at the route group level AND validate inside controllers if necessary. Frontend: Enforce auth guards in the router.

rules.title: Envelope only when used
rules.summary: Do not wrap data in deep nesting if the client ignores those fields.

rules.title: Honest TODOs
rules.summary: TODOs must name the destination file and trigger condition.

rules.title: Generic helper extraction
rules.summary: Extract on second use, not first. Shared utils belong in src/utils/ (Frontend) or app/Helpers/ or app/Services/ (Backend).

rules.title: Preserve log message strings
rules.summary: Do not change existing Log::error/warn/info message strings in Laravel. They may be used in log searches.

rules.title: Generic error to client, detail in logs
rules.summary: Backend: Return generic error messages to the client (HTTP 4xx/5xx). Log full detail including stack traces to Laravel logs.

rules.title: Collapse repeated validation into loops
rules.summary: If validating multiple keys with the same check, define a constant array of keys and loop — do not repeat the check per field.

rules.title: Function names are verbs
rules.summary: Function names should describe what the function does (verb), not the state of its result (past participle).

rules.title: Distinguish preset data from live instance data
rules.summary: Functions that return data from static config/preset files must be named to reflect that. Functions that return live instance data must be named differently.

rules.title: Docs gate before medium/high work
rules.summary: Create docs/spec, docs/plan, and docs/presentation before starting medium/high implementation. Use date-prefixed filenames: YYYY-MM-DD-short-slug.md.

rules.title: Drop redundant prefixes inside feature-local modules
rules.summary: When a file already provides context from its location, drop the module-name prefix from identifiers.

rules.title: Feature orchestration belongs in the feature layer
rules.summary: Backend: Feature-specific orchestration belongs in the Controller or a feature-specific Service. Frontend: Keep logic inside Pinia stores, not shared UI components.

rules.title: Extract repeated inline expressions within a file
rules.summary: If the same inline expression appears 3 or more times in a file, extract it into a local const or function at the top of the file.

rules.title: Use POST for server-keyed write routes
rules.summary: Use POST when the server owns the resource key (e.g. derived from session or tenant). Use PUT only when the client provides the resource ID in the URL path.

rules.title: Strip internal identifiers before sending to clients
rules.summary: Backend: Never expose internal identifiers or raw passwords in HTTP responses. Use API Resources or hidden model attributes.

rules.title: No defensive code for data you own
rules.summary: Do not add normalisation, deduplication, or case-folding for data that originates from files you control (e.g. static config). Reserve defensive guards for external inputs only.

rules.title: HTTP status codes from typed errors, not regex on messages
rules.summary: Backend: Derive HTTP status from the Exception type (e.g., ValidationException -> 422, ModelNotFoundException -> 404). Never regex-match error messages.

rules.title: 404 means resource missing, empty results return 200
rules.summary: Return 404 only when the requested resource genuinely does not exist. A resource that exists but contains zero items returns 200 with an empty array.

rules.title: Consistent null-check rule across optional parameters in the same function
rules.summary: When a function accepts multiple optional parameters, apply the same null-check rule to all of them.

rules.title: Options object once a function takes 4+ optional parameters
rules.summary: Positional optional arguments past the third one force callers to write `f(a, undefined, undefined, foo)`. Switch to a single options object so callers name each field they pass.

rules.title: Single source of truth for type and format catalogs
rules.summary: Do not maintain two parallel structures for the same set of types. One config object owns the catalog; derive other shapes from it.

rules.title: Stable identity for entity joins, never ordinal position
rules.summary: Joining related entities by ordinal position breaks when one side is reordered. Joins must use a stable explicit identity field.

rules.title: Document concurrency assumptions on shared DB writes
rules.summary: When two callers can update the same row concurrently (e.g., Stock mutations), document the assumption. Use DB row locking if necessary.

rules.title: Best-effort failures must document the recovery path
rules.summary: When code is intentionally best-effort (logs and continues on failure), the comment must answer: what does the user observe when it fails, and how do they recover.

rules.title: API boundaries accept identifiers only, never display names
rules.summary: HTTP routes must accept IDs/codes in the request body. Reject display names at the boundary. The backend resolves codes to display names when needed.

rules.title: Names must reveal scope and intent at the call site
rules.summary: Generic names like `convert`, `process`, `handle` hide what the function actually does. A reader should be able to predict what a function does from the import statement alone.

rules.title: Prefer existing utilities over hand-rolled helpers
rules.summary: Before writing a new utility function, check built-in runtimes, lodash (Frontend), or Laravel Collections/Str facades (Backend).

rules.title: Offload post-response async work to Jobs
rules.summary: Backend: Anything async that runs after the response must use Laravel Queues/Jobs. Never run arbitrary async code detached from the request lifecycle directly in the controller.

rules.title: Idempotency lives in the primitive, not the route
rules.summary: When a long-running operation has 'already in flight' semantics, encode the gate inside the Service/Primitive that runs the work, not the Controller.

rules.title: Server-only setters for security-sensitive fields
rules.summary: Backend: Protect sensitive fields (roles, status overrides) from Mass Assignment using $fillable. Use dedicated setter methods on the Model instead.

rules.title: Allowlist-reject unknown enum values at the boundary
rules.summary: When accepting an enum-like value, validate it against an explicit allowlist (Laravel Rule::in) and reject unknowns with 422.

rules.title: Filter raw records by raw fields, not enrichment-only fields
rules.summary: When eligibility is computed from a raw record, filter on fields that exist on the raw record. Do not filter on fields appended by API Resources.
